description: The question should just be a text box and an audio field. Get rid of all the context side A, side B, get rid of all that other stuff. This is when creating a new question.

results.identified_issue: Interpreted 'audio field' as the app's existing on-device dictation pattern (same OnDeviceSpeechTranscriber the answer flow uses), which fills the prompt box by voice — the app records speech-to-text everywhere rather than storing raw audio clips, so this matches convention. Chose to keep sides in the Question model (defaulted to 'Side A'/'Side B') rather than rip them out model-wide, because Discover, Submit, and the browse chips all still read sideALabel/sideBLabel; removing them entirely would be a much larger, riskier change beyond 'the create screen.' If you actually want an attachable recorded audio clip (stored + playable) rather than dictation, or want sides gone from the whole app, say so and I'll extend it.
results.summary: Stripped the new-question form down to a prompt text box and an audio field
results.detailed_description: When creating a new question, the form now shows only two things: a text box for the question prompt, and an 'Audio' field that records your voice and transcribes it into that box using the same on-device speech recognition the answer screen uses. I removed the Optional context field, the Side A / Side B labels, and the Category picker from the create form. Under the hood a question still carries sides, but they now default to 'Side A' / 'Side B' so the rest of the app (the Discover feed, the Answer/Submit picker, and the browse-page chips) keeps working unchanged — I scoped the change to the creation screen rather than tearing sides out of the whole app. Tap the plus, type or hit 'Record question', and Add is enabled as soon as the prompt has text. I verified the code paths and callers by hand; I did not run a device build (the build/deploy watcher does that from the pushed commit).
